entities.name: Julija Borja
entities.body_md: Ambitious Marshal of the Federal Republic, she is a by the book official and finds it difficult operating in a company controlled system. Her lack of direct authority outside of limited areas forces her to spend most time doing paperwork reporting actions of the company, as well as arranging the deportations from the Thides System. She is effectively the only representative of the Federal Republic in the system.

relationships.role: Member of Marshal of the Federal Republic

entities.name: Solveig Kaufer
entities.body_md: Assistant to the Station Manager of MacMillian IV, she is the main person running the station as her boss Wilton offloaded his work on her to add to her work total. Because of this overt trust by Wilton, Solveig is given a free hand running the station. While she might be the ultimate power of the station, she has gone out of her way to run the station efficiently and fairly. All actions appear reasoned and justified, and bringing no rebuke from corporate or the feds.

relationships.role: Member of MacMillian Mining and Extraction Corporation

entities.name: Holger Graeme
entities.body_md: Chief of station security, he's trying his best to keep the station and system safe and ordered as possible, even if it's a hopeless task.

relationships.role: Leader of Station Security (MacMillian Station VI)

entities.name: Samina Ventura
entities.body_md:
A retired prostitute that once worked in some of the highest class pleasure houses in the Federal Republic, Samina took her life savings and bought The Silk Abbey on MacMillian Station IV. She attempted to raise the quality, and the class, but there is only so much to do with the station in the state it is. She manages to attract the best girls on the station, with a higher level of cliental, and fairer operating fees.

She maintains a special relation with the Station Manager, and this allows her to quietly offer more risky services for more income. This includes looking the other ways as the dealers ply their drugs in the establishment, provided she gets a kickback. Station security is on to her, but as long as the illegal activities are confined to The Silk Abbey, Samina has nothing to worry about as security has other bigger problems.

entities.name: Roland Mohamed
entities.body_md:
One time wildcatter, Roland found more success in running The Comet's Tail than any mining claim he ever worked. No one knows how he came to own The Comet's Tail, but they do know it's officially his. His laissez-faire attitude towards patron's actions makes his establishment one of the more lively on the station. He has only two rules, don't annoy him and don't bring the cops. Anyone breaking those rules will soon find themselves on the other end of the pickaxe he keeps behind the bar.

Roland once served as a marine in the Federal Military where he lost his arms. He's never been clear as to how he lost them, the story changes every time he tells it.

entities.name: Vita Ó Taidhg
entities.body_md:
Docking chief of MacMillian Station IV, she has worked for the company all her life, and wants to look out for her "boys" on the dock. Nothing gets on/off the station without her signoff, or at least a confirmation. Her concern for the future of the company, and the job at the station leaves her worried.

Five years ago she lead a strike of the dock workers that got them better pay. Since then the company has atrophied her force by not replacing retires, fired, or quitting workers. This would lead to her often behind schedule in getting the shipments out, if the number of ships didn't decrease over the years. It has, however, made her less able to be as thorough as she wants, something the more opportunistic have taken advantage of for both legal and illegal means.

relationships.role: Member of MacMillian Mining and Extraction Corporation

entities.name: Zigurds Sloan
entities.body_md:
Ziguads is the man to find if you have a mechanical problem on the station. He knows most of the inner working of the station, and it's equipment, as well as some other things. He's become jaded over the years due to what he perceives as a lack of respect of his talents by the powers that be at the MacMillian corporation. As such he's been known to use his knowledge of the station to line his pockets and to do off book work for the right price. Security won't touch him as he's the only man keeping things running around here.

He's been in a relationship with Solveig Kaufer for the last year,

He is a degenerate gambler and can be found often during his time off in some of the entertainment district establishments that offer gambling. It's suspected he's in debt to some of the establishments, and even some of the less reputable residents of the station.

entities.name: Opeyemi Tichá
entities.body_md: Criminal boss, and owner of the Nova Nexus, Opeyemi has their hand in many of the more illegal operations in the system. He's most suspected of arranging the shipments of Wildcatter production out of the system without having paid the company fees, netting himself and his clients a nice additional profit. In addition to his "business" ventures, he holds influence in the Serene Syndicate, an inter-steller criminal organization.

entities.name: Asher Bhattacharya
entities.body_md: Leader of one of the two crews of the Syndicate in the Thides system, he is more of the muscle fo the boss. Concerned with the protection, debt collection, and other forms of intimidation if Asher shows up lookind for you it'll probably be a bad time

entities.name: Puleng Huang
entities.body_md: An underling of Opeyemi in the Thides System, the trickier of the two. She joined the Syndicate as a teenager in the Bellerophon System. After too many run ins with the local government law enforcement due to her hot temper, she had to flee to avoid arrest and imprisonment. Escaping into the Federal Republic, she bounced around between syndicate groups until landing in the Thides system. Here Opeyemi found her after an introduction by on of his "friends". Opeyemi found her penchant for violence to be useful. While not as strong and physically imposing as many members of the Syndicate, the ability to be crafty makes her more deadly.

entities.name: Surendra Antonescu
entities.body_md:
A priest of the Orthodox faith that services the needy on MacMillian Station IV. He's taken to multiple acts to being the end to the suffering of the downtrodden of the station. While he will maintain the formal aspects of his position by holding regular mass, and offering the other required sacraments, most of the time is spent on missions of service throughout the station.

Rumor is some action in his past lead to his assignment to this bleak outpost. His actions back this up, making many that know of the faith to believe he is seeking redemption for some past action. Before his arrival only the occasional passing priest of the Orthodox faith would pass through the system, usually at the request of some resident for a specific reason (last rights, marriage, burial, etc.

relationships.role: Owner of Church of Saint Barbara

entities.name: Khasan Stamp
entities.body_md:
Owner and operator of Stamp Supply, he's managed to corner the market in supplying most of those still operating in the Thides System by either muscling out the competition or the disinterest in business on a failing station. He is known as the biggest fence in the station, and will usually buy anything of value. Station security has yet to figure out how he manages to get the items off station, but believes he might have an agreement with Opeyemi to facilitate this.

While his connections allow him to move items off station and out of the system, he lacks the ability to smuggle items in. It is unknown if this is due to some inability, or a lack of desire to conduct that sort of business.

entities.name: Kasey Garnett
entities.body_md:
Owner and chief of Garnett's Reach, she runs the largest illegal outpost in the Thides System. Space in her outpost is rented to different individuals to conduct their own operations. There is one rule, no strangers at the outpost. Because of this most of those operating out of the reach act only as middle men in transactions, arranging the exchange of goods between to parties. This can be inter and intra system deals.

Another offer by Kasey is her ability to acquire the swarm loading key codes. Allowing people to hide shipments arriving/leaving the system in the swarm load. She offers this access for a few, if you can get in contact with her.

entities.name: Alena González
entities.body_md: OmniStar Broadcasting reporter in the Thides System. They are working a lead on a story about Pratrock Dynamics founder, and their start as a subcontractor to MacMillian Mining and Extraction Corporation in the Thides System.

entities.name: Ihsan Soterios
entities.body_md: Second in command and muscle of the Rusted Blades. Ihsan grew up in the slums of Processor I. After meeting Leocadia he found more opportunities in her company.  He is fiercely loyal to Leocadia, as she pulled him out of poverty. While he appears to play the part of dumb brute, there is a hidden cunning in his actions

entities.name: Aco Brownlow
entities.body_md: A wildcatter currently located in the Thides system. He's spent his years illegally mining and jumping claims, being banned from numerious systems. Chasing the "mother lode" that will bring a fortune.

entities.name: Leocadia Zavia
entities.body_md:
Leader of the Rusted Blades, she grew up in the system in the slums of Processor I. She formed her gang originally by running protection rackets among the businesses on the station, threatening that the kids in her gang would disrupt them. Eventually she managed to scrape enough script together to purchase a run down ship. With the ship, with her gang, managed to extend their racket to the whole system, targeting unlicensed mining operations.

She is smart enough to stay under MacMillian security. Occasionally tipping them off about some operations, usually those that wont pay.

entities.name: Vanadís Baumhauer
entities.body_md:
A resident of Processing I, Vanadís grew up on the station. Her mother long since disappeared, and her father spends most of his time off station working for whoever will take him. In the meantime Vanadís grew up in the underclass of the station, learning tricks and scams to survive. Holger Graeme's arrested her a few times, but she always has a bit of information to trade, something worth more than the fine or jail time she is facing. Opeyemi's banned her from his businesses, as well as told his crews to steer clear of her, having his operations effected by her information trades. For the right price, Vanadís can get you any piece of information you need.

You can usually find her on the Main Line of Processing I, working angles, trading information, and making deals.

entities.name: Ajeet Siegel
entities.body_md: Owner of the scanning operation named after him, he runs this one man show. Ajeet's been willing to take a percentage of the operation instead of pay. This gives him an extra income source, but ties him to the market. More so than other operations like his.

entities.name: Pia Kadyrov
entities.body_md: Pia is a small time reporter and content distributor in the Thides System. She reports on the local news as best she can, while trying to find a big story that will get her recognized by the bigger networks. She once worked as an intern for FRNC, but left after her opportunities for promotion dried up, at least that is the story she tells. A few years ago she came to Thides and setup her operation, more that she just took over the local abandoned setup that the company ignored for decades.

entities.name: Thandeka Dahl
entities.body_md: The operator of the salvage ship "The Beloved Valley" in the Thides System. Like a bunch of operators, she has moved to the system to hope to profit off the collapse of the MacMillian company. She spends most of her time looking for old abandoned mining operations to salvage.

entities.name: Wilton Agramunt
entities.body_md:
Current manager of MacMilian Station IV, and director in the Macmillian company. He sees his job as a dead end, and just hopes the company survives long enough for him to retire. Oblivious to the goings on around the station, he leans more on his directory secretary to run the day to day operations. Little or none of the station business reach him, leaving him not only obtuse, but also mis-informed about what is going on around him.

He always dresses impeccably, as a company director of a larger more successful corporation would, far outside someone of his current status would.

relationships.role: Station Manager of MacMillian Station VI
Member of MacMillian Mining and Extraction Corporation

entities.name: MacMillian Mining and Extraction Corporation
entities.body_md: A once mighty corporation fallen on hard times. From the highpoint of operations in almost every connected system, they have fallen on hard times. Scraping out what value they can extract from the few remaining outposts they control.

entities.name: Marshal of the Federal Republic
entities.body_md: The most common federal official people have interactions with, they represent the law and order of the Federal Republic. There will be at least one assigned to every occupied system in the Federal Republic. Marshals are authorized to deputize locals to serve under them as needed. In addition, Marshals are authorized to enforce federal law even against the local authorities. When a citizen is arrested by local authorities, one of the first contacts is the local Marshals.

entities.name: MacMillian Security
entities.body_md: A subsidiary of MacMillian Mining and Extraction company, they are responsible for the security of all the company's operations.

entities.name: Station Security (MacMillian Station VI)
entities.body_md: Station security for MacMillian Station VI. They are responsible for not only station security, but also the system security. They are extremely understaffed for the area they cover. This is due not just to budget constraints, but also for the lack of candidate who want to take jobs with no future. The security will take any applicant, even ignoring potential issues just to fill our numbers.

entities.name: Pratrock Dynamics
entities.body_md: A new corporation on the block, from humble beginnings the company has managed to secure some high value contracts with the government with their technical innovations. Their first offering, a sensor update allowing something in physical space to detect physical object in hyperspace has been a boon to gate operations.

entities.name: Carrick-Malik Armament Group
entities.body_md: A large armament manufacturer, they are known to build the best point defense systems.

entities.name: Steiger Aerospace
entities.body_md: Builder of the Steiger Bulk Cargo Hauler Tug, the military Strider class atmospheric enabled transport ship, and the military Sentinel class corvette. Their expertise is in building rugged simplistic ships for the military. Because of this, many people look to purchase decommissioned Strider and Sentinel ships since their chassis offer a customizable platform.

entities.name: Serene Syndicate
entities.body_md:
An Inter-steller criminal loosely affiliated organization. Their name is given to them by the Marshal service due to their quiet and unassuming nature as there is no recorded structure or knowledge of membership. Members know of who inducted them and any one else they know of. To meet a member they don't personally know, they need to be introduced by a member that knows both them and the new party. This rule has made it difficult for the Marshals to break their influence, as it's hard to get informants into the organization. Even those that do manage, don't often come into contact with more than a few members.

The organization is divided up into many independent operating groups that have little or no contact with one another. Each group usually controls a territory. These territories can be entires systems, or just a city block. They depend on the size of the group, and the boss's influence. Each group is lead by a boss. That boss usually has a number of underlings. These underlings usually lead a crew. Sometimes the boss leads a crew, but this usually only occurs if the boss's group is only a few members. Often a boss will not know all the members of the group, they usually just know the underlings. At the bottom the crew members will only know their leader, who they assume is the boss. If their leader is actually an underling, they don't often know who the boss is. Groups names are often labeled by some feature of the territory they control.

Disputes between Syndicate members can be hard to resolve. First, most time those in dispute don't know the other is a member. Second, that often they don't have a shared member that they trust to resolve this disputes. Because wars between groups are bad for business, many of the more powerful bosses take care to get introductions to anyone in neighboring groups and their bosses. In additon there are many symbols the Syndicate uses to mark out a territory is occupied by another group.

entities.name: Thides Serene Syndicate
entities.body_md: A group of the Serene Syndicate that operates in the Thides system

entities.name: Orthodox Apostolic Church
entities.body_md:
A faith founded on Thocronoe IV, one of the core foundational planets of the Federal Republic. The faith is a monotheistic religion based on the belief of one universal creator. This creator is responsible for the entire universe and all that resides in it. The faith teaches that the creator designed the universe to be self sustaining so no direct interference is needed. However, they are taught that through indirect means the creator will resolve issues they see.

Due to their beliefs, members find a need to seek signs of the creator's workings through the actions of others, and the actions of nature around them. Believers are supposed to understand their fallibility in interpreting these signs, but this is often disregarded by schismatic and heretical sects. Members are taught to that the importance is not in the reason of these signs, but in seeing them and understanding the creators ongoing plan. These actions represent that the creator continues to perfect his creation, and that no future is determined because of these changes.

entities.name: Federal Republican Swarm Network
entities.body_md: This is a swarm network run by the Federal Republic, and largest swarm network. It is available in every publicly accessible system in the republic. Membership keys are controlled by federal authorities, or license local representatives.

entities.name: OmniStar Broadcasting
entities.body_md:
The largest news network in the Federal Republic. OmniStar operates numerous stations and broadcasting platforms throughout the Federal Republic. Stations can vary, from a full operation with their own reporters and on air personalities, to a simple repeater setup to broadcast reports it receives from  other locations

Since information can only flow as fast as a ship travels, or in extreme cases the speed it can traverse the hyperspace lanes, the company operates mostly as a local operation. News about the local system the station is operating in usually leads the reports. Republic wide news is usually a delayed report read by the anchors, or a pre-recorded feature shared across the network, but out of date.

The network employs a number of independent correspondents and reporters over the Federal Republic to fill out it's operation. These reporters are paid by the viewership on a story, so they are more apt to look for reports that will carry the largest audience. The network is required to carry all Federal Republic Bulletins, these commonly take less of a focus than the popular sensational news.

entities.name: Rusted Blades
entities.body_md:
A criminal gang that operates in the Thides System. They mostly scam wildcatter operations for protection money, threatening to wreck their operation, or inform on them to MacMillian. Most will just pay the "tax" but a few resist, till they are given the option to pay or get spaced. The gang operate below the radar of MacMillian security as they steer clear of interfering with the company, and it's licensees.

When not out running their protection, or hunting down new marks, they can be found at The Comet's Tail.

Kasey Garnett holds a particular grudge against the gang, as their antics threaten clients of hers, and she fears they might turn to MacMillian for protection. The fear is that they sell her out as well.

entities.name: Horizon Systems
entities.body_md: A conglomerate over numerous companies that uses their integration to out compete and crush their competitors.

entities.name: Horizon Aerospace
entities.body_md: The aerospace company under the Horizon Systems umbrella. They create and design upper mid tier spaceships. Their ships are less rugged but have a more visually pleasing design. They are know to offer more comforts as part of their ship construction, such as showers, full galleys, and private rooms.

entities.name: Ajeet Scanning
entities.body_md: A company based in the Thides sector, it's a one man operation. Focused mostly on asteroid scanning, the company earns by finding the raw materials the miners seek. The company operates one ship that they hire out to scan asteroids, and the occasional moon, for materials their clients request. While most companies want their full fee up front, they have been known to take partial or no-fee and a percent of the recovery. This has allowed the company to grow their funds quicker that expected, but is always one bad result from bankruptcy.

entities.name: Federal Republican News Corporation
entities.body_md:
A public/private corporation that operates under the FRBS as a nationwide clearing house for news. The FRNC is setup to broadcast 24/7 with offices on many worlds and stations. FRNC will often contract out on the ground reporting from local resources, then use their special relation with the FRBS to deliver the reports across the network.

Due to the governmental nature of the organization, the FRNC content is managed by strict rules setup by the Federal Republic. While there is no formal censorship rules, there are unstated agreements the FRNC maintains with the government.

entities.name: Thides Cast
entities.body_md: A small operation run by Pia Kadyrov in the Thides system. Mostly run out of her domicile, the operation is scraping by on mostly re-broadcast content from the major networks.

entities.name: Federal Republican Broadcasting Service
entities.body_md:
A broadcast network setup by the federal republic using the communication network established by the republic to send secure and non-secure messages across space. To finance the construction of the network for official and military use, the republic granted concessions to broadcasters to transmit across the network for set fees. These transmissions can be network wide or limited to regional areas. Fees are determined by the distance and area covered.

Local planetary broadcasts are governed by the local planetary laws, this network only regulates cross space fast transit. The network uses a series of direct beam transmissions that transmit signals from beacon to beacon in both normal and hyperspace. Speed of the transmission is limited by the speed of light, so these signals often take weeks to the outer rim of human civilization.

Broadcasters will pay the FRBS to transmit content. This content then is received by local factors licensed and authorized to then transmit this data. Some of these can be multi system groups, while others can be a small company operation on a lonely spacestation.

entities.name: Sector 7-G
entities.body_md: A sector of the Federal Republic located on the rim of occupied space, it's know for its many dead end systems.

entities.name: MacMillian Station IV
entities.body_md:
The only remaining station owned by the MacMillian Mining and Extraction Corporation from their once mighty fleet of operations. It's located in the Thides system, and continues to operate along with their operations in the system. The station has seen better times, but it still general operational. The station contains:

Landing hangers for smaller ships
Docking hookups for large ships
Services for ships (repair and fueling)
Entertainment district
Processing/Shipping factory for mining operations in the system
Re-supply options for subcontractor and wildcatters working in this system
Residences for station staff and visitors
Security offices for the company
Fed marshal office

entities.name: The Silk Abbey
entities.body_md: On of the most popular drinking establishment on MacMillian Station VI, its a cover for the brothel that earns the madame Samina Ventura the most of her income. Each "private" room is named to try to bring it some high class status, but this place would be a low quality establishment if not for the other company it keeps.

entities.name: Nova Nexus
entities.body_md: A gambling establishment and nightclub located on MacMillian Station IV. It is owned by the biggest known criminal boss in the sector, Opeyemi Tichá. While intended to be a front operation, it's surprise popularity on the station makes it as valueable as any other of Opeyemi's enterprises.While considered upscale, its just as dirty as any other establishment on the station, this just has music, neon, and a dance floor.

entities.name: Church of Saint Barbara
entities.body_md: A chapel located on the MacMillian Station IV, it is a small space built out of a former residence. The chapel is a place of refuge for members of the Orthodox religion. Setup by Surendra Antonescu after his arrival at the station a decade ago, it acts as the central hub for his outreach. While he will preform masses in the chapel as required, or requested, there are few regular attendees.

entities.name: Stamp Supply
entities.body_md: Owned and operated by Khasan Stamp, it is the major source of supplies for those conducting operations in the system. They are a licenses subcontractor of MacMillian, but are known to conduct "off book" operations.

entities.name: Garnett's Reach
entities.body_md:
A "secret" outpost in the Thidian Belt that operates clandestine operations under the company's nose. While formally under Kasey Garnett's rule, the place is more of a collection of independent operators running their own enterprises. MacMillian knows of it's existence in the system, but is unable to determine it's location.

People generally don't visit the station. Instead they will contact and meetup somewhere in the system with representatives of those that have access to the outpost. These representatives are authorized to complete deals, and arrange the exchange of goods and currency. It is rare that items from these deals are brought back to the outpost, more often they are shipped on to the next person in the chain. Those operating out of the outpost more often act as middle men in the transactions, than actual direct dealers.

entities.name: Federal Republic of Worlds
entities.body_md:
The Federal Republic of Worlds, or Federal Republic, is a representative state that governs over all occupied space. The Federal Republic controls all of the space between member worlds, as well as any unoccupied places.

Member worlds all select by their own method members to the Federal Senate, while proportioned district of citizens exist for the elections of members to a commons. While the senate and commons control most of the governance of the Republic, final say rests with the Council. The Council is made up of eleven worlds (seven founding worlds, and four additions) that hold veto power over the decisions of the senate and commons.

Members of the Federal Republic are granted freedom to govern their territory as they see fit, provided they don't violate any federal laws or the protections given citizens. Any member world found in violation can and will be blockaded by the other member worlds until such time they return to compliance. In extreem cases Federal Military will intervene.

Inter world commerce is the main item under the purview of the Federal Republic. Any ship traveling between worlds is subject to taxes or fees, the most common being the "jump tax" placed on the use of gates. Each route has a rate that is tolled to ships traveling that path. In addition the Federal Republic issues licenses to people/corporations for operations in space, including in places not occupied by a member world.

Citizenship is an important aspect to the Federal Republic. Every citizen is protected by the Federal Republic the same, regardless of their allegiance to any member world. Federal law applies first to any citizen, even if it contradicts as member world's laws. Citizens are even able to appeal to Federal authorities in disputes with member worlds. Not all people are citizens. To be a citizen you must be a child of a citizen or have served in the military/government. You may revoke your citizenship at any time. Member worlds my not execute a citizen, the only executable charge for the Military is treason, at most they can deport and deny entry to you. Citizens also possess the freedom of movement, in that in most cases they can travel without restrictions in the Federal Republic.

For those that are not citizens are considered residents when operating outside their home world. They have no protections of the Federal Republic, and if found in violation of any other member laws, or Federal, they will be deported to their home world and denied the right to leave their home world, in addition to any punishment they are subject to based on the laws they violated. Non citizens may have their restriction limited for any reason to any member world, or the Federal Republic, they must be accepted by their home world if deported.

entities.name: The Comet's Tail
entities.body_md: One time the best establishment on the station, its now home to some of the roughest individuals in the system. Operated by Roland Mohamed, it's place you'll leave with a hangover, a bloody nose, and no money.

entities.name: Processing I
entities.body_md:
First of the four processing plants located in the lower section of the station. This plant is the first to go online during the station's construction. After the almost literal collapse of mining operations on Thides I, the company decided to mothball the plant as the other newer processing plants could keep up with demand.

Overtime, some of the more industrious and desperate manages to break in. At first they planned only to salvage and steal any thing of value in the plant to sell on the black market. Eventually a few more desperate people began to reside in the remains of the plant. This led to the creation of it's own social framework.

Station operations and security largely ignores it, as the location is useful to keep out the poor and forgotten out of the main parts of the station, and the option to ignore the demands of supporting this underclass. Operations will only bother with the area if one of the locals causes some issue. Such as illegally tapping into station system for access to power or coms. Security does occasional sweeps, but mostly limits their involvement. Occasionally security is required to escort an operations team or hunt down a wanted criminal.

The area has built up its only society and economy mostly from blackmarket dealings. People find it easier to conduct clandestine and illegal endeavors here, out of the direct view. This means anyone looking for something less than legal is likely to find it here. The population is a mix of crashed out old timers, people with legitimate jobs looking for less expensive accommodation, drug addicts, street criminals, destitute, etc. It is very similar to the slums you might find on any large planetary city.

entities.name: Docking Ring
entities.body_md: The outermost ring of the station, the docking ring is where most of the ships arrive at the station. Smaller ships can fit into the hangers that allow the area around them to be pressurized. Larger ships need to attach to docking rings, and look to drones or lurkers to hook service them. The ring is broken into two parts, the public section that is accessible to the public, and the corporate section that is limited to company personal. The public section is less active, mostly being used by people operating their own ships in the system. There is limited inter-system traffic, mostly supply ships delivering items not able to be produced in system, and a few daily transports offering passage in/out of the system.The corporate system is mostly built around the processing and shipping of the extracted resources from the system. These are loaded onto large transport trains, and sent out to their pre-planned destinations. While much of the dock's operations are automated, the age of the station and equipment leads to many breakdowns, and a need for a much larger human staff than normal

entities.name: The Pinnacle
entities.body_md: A modified Diamon Dynamics Mark XII Service Tug used by the Rusted Blades for their operations in the Thides System

entities.name: The Main Line
entities.body_md:
The one time main path of the processing facility, it has become a sort of main street to the slums of MacMillian Station IV. Over the years people carved out places for stores along what used to be conveyer belts carrying the ore to be processed by the plant. You can find a mix of people selling legitimate or illegitimate product along its length.

While it's not a place to find the more formal members of society, those people that scrape out survival on the station it's their home. Some of those on the station, as well as those from the nearby system, will frequently visit the Main Line looking for a deal.

entities.name: Thides Gate
entities.body_md: The hyperspace gate out of the system. It leads to Corwin-Thides route

entities.name: Corwin Gate III
entities.body_md: The third gate in the Corwin system, this gate leads to the Corwin-Thides Route

entities.name: Pratrock Dynamics Research Facility 42
entities.body_md: A research facility build into the crust of the rocky planet Corwin II.

entities.name: Eris Gate IV
entities.body_md: The fourth gate out of the Eris system that leads to the Corwin-Eris Route

entities.name: Corwin Gate II
entities.body_md: A gate that leads to the Corwin-Eris route.

entities.name: Aethel
entities.body_md: A city and starport that exists on the planet Eris III

entities.name: 07-Delta Station
entities.body_md: One of the last mining operation on Thides I that operated until the planet almost reached planetary collapse. The operation was abandoned by the MacMillian Company and left to rot, without maintance the solar winds, heat, and radiation began to break down the remains. Some of the facilities would be operational with a bit of effort, and some remain still pressure sealed and safe to inhabit. Scavengers will occasionally seek out the location to harvest for supplies and equipment left behind.

entities.name: The Beloved Valley
entities.body_md: A Orion Class Salvage Ship operated by Thandeka Dahl in the Thides System

entities.name: Thides System
entities.body_md: A dead end system with one jump route charted in/out of the system. It is primarily owned by the MacMillian Mining and Extraction Corporation for purposes of exploiting any and all resources found in the system. The MacMillian operates the primary processing, shipping, and management station in the system. All current exploiting operations are managed by subcontractors to MacMillian. MacMillian maintains control by managing travel to/from the system.

entities.name: Thides
entities.body_md:
Type: K5 V Orange Main Sequence

Main and only star of the Thides System.

entities.name: Thides II
entities.body_md: A Terrestrial world with a thick corrosive atmosphere, the planet itself has not been mined, even though it is open to access. This is mostly due to the cost of maintenance on equipment and the lack of quality surveys of surface. Most current prospecting returned less than optimal results, even if an occasional wildcatter will make an attempt. It is considered a level 3, dangerous but safe for access with the correct equipment

entities.name: Thides III
entities.body_md: A habitable terrestrial world that possesses a breathable atmosphere, Thides III is unoccupied by being declared a restricted planet, due to dangerous flora and fauna. Many of the creatures, and some of the plant, have a natural affinity for electromagnetic waves. These seem to draw the flora and fauna toward sources of the waves. This draw, along with the deadly nature of these creatures, makes establishment of any human activity and difficult, leading Federal scientists to place restrictions on the plant. It is considered a level 1 deadly restrictions due to the flora and fauna.

entities.name: Thidian Belt
entities.body_md: An asteroid belt and one of the two currently actively mined areas of the system. The belt consists of a number of large and small objects that many wildcatters and small subcontractors extract minerals from. Most are low deposit sources, making it unproductive for larger operations. It is located between Thiden III and Thiden IV.

entities.name: Thides IV
entities.body_md: A gas giant with multiple moons. The strength of the gravity around the planet, and it's strong wind, makes is impractical to harvest gas from. The remaining large mining operations by the company in the system exist among it's moons.

entities.name: Thides VI
entities.body_md: Ice planet with high radiation levels

entities.name: Thides V
entities.body_md: An ice planet with trace atmosphere.

entities.name: Corwin-Thides Route
entities.body_md: A path between the Corwin and Thides Systems

entities.name: Corwin System
entities.body_md: A Binary start system with no habitable planets, the system possesses three gates

entities.name: Corwin I
entities.body_md: A tidally locked, airless rocky world heavily cratered by meteorites.

entities.name: Corwin III
entities.body_md: A large gas giant with fifteen moons, as well as an impressive set of rings. Three of the moons contain non-breathable, but harmless atmosphere. One of these moons is occupied by a settlement

entities.name: Corwin IV
entities.body_md: An giant frozen gas giant

entities.name: Corwin B
entities.body_md: A cooler, orange K-type star (0.7 solar masses).

entities.name: Corwin V
entities.body_md: A dwarf planet one the outer edge of the system, it has an irregular orbit in that it's more oval with an extreem difference between it's narrowest and furthest distance to the center of the system.

entities.name: Corwin A
entities.body_md: A bright yellow-white F-type star (1.3 solar masses) providing the bulk of the system's heat and light.

entities.name: Corwin II
entities.body_md: The second and larger rocky world. It has trace atmosphere in some of the craters, but not enough to maintain life. Pratrock Dynamics maintains a secure research facility here.

entities.name: Corwin III C
entities.body_md: Third moon of Corwin III, it is the second of the planet's moons with a majority carbon dioxide atmosphere.

entities.name: Corwin III A
entities.body_md: Closest moon of Corwin III, and largest. It has a high carbon dioxide, non-breathable atmosphere. The moon close proximity to the large Corwin III causes gravitational effects to the planet, making it's surface tectonics highly unstable.

entities.name: Corwin III D
entities.body_md: The fourth planet orbiting Corwin III, it is home to a settlement. The settlement provides residences and breaks to the workers of the nearby Pratrock Research Facility in the system, as well as support for travelers through the system. There is no space elevator, but the reduced gravity of the planet (.75G) makes it more cost effective to launch ships off the surface to provide services. The moon's atmosphere contains oxygen, but not enough to breath without assistance, most people will use breathing assistant setups when walking around the surface, and most of the settlement is a sealed environment.

entities.name: Corwin-Eris Route
entities.body_md: The eldest path into the Corwin System, it runs from the Eris System.

entities.name: Eris A
entities.body_md: Orange K type star

entities.name: Erisian Belt
entities.body_md:
A massive planetary ring system created billions of years ago when two large ice moons collided. It is densely packed with water ice and precious metals. It is located between Eris IV and Eris V

The primary dwarf planet within the belt has deep, vacuum-exposed chasms that are actively mined

entities.name: Eris VI
entities.body_md:
A massive outer planet primarily composed of ammonia, methane, and water ice. It possesses a dark, thin ring system and 14 captured moons.
It has intense, planet-spanning auroras at its poles that are visible even from deep space.

entities.name: Eris I
entities.body_md: A former gas giant that had its thick atmosphere completely stripped away by intense stellar winds. It is now a molten ball of heavy metals and exposed iron cores. Experiences severe tidal locking, with a permanent day-side glowing bright red.

entities.name: Eris IV
entities.body_md: A cold, dry planet covered in fine iron oxide dust, featuring massive canyons and extinct shield volcanoes. It has two small, potato-shaped moons that orbit so close to the surface they cross the sky in just a few hours.

entities.name: Eris II
entities.body_md:
Locked in a runaway greenhouse effect, this world features crushing atmospheric pressure and a thick yellow fog of vaporized sulfur dioxide.

Intense friction in the lower atmosphere creates perpetual, global sheet lightning storms that never hit the ground.

entities.name: Eris V
entities.body_md: A massive gas giant composed almost entirely of hydrogen and helium, featuring bands of striking copper, orange, and cream clouds. Possesses a chaotic system of 42 moons, three of which share the exact same orbital path in a stable configuration.

entities.name: Eris VII
entities.body_md: Positioned at the very edge of the Orange Dwarf's light, this world is a freezing wasteland of nitrogen ice, methane snow, and jagged obsidian-like rock formations. Its highly eccentric orbit occasionally pulls it inside the path of Eris VI, causing temporary atmospheric thaws.

entities.name: Eris III
entities.body_md:
The habitable zone world of the system. This planet is 1.4 times the mass of Earth and covered primarily by shallow, mineral-rich purple oceans and massive equatorial rainforests.

It has a highly reflective, dense atmosphere that creates a constant twilight glow rather than a harsh, direct daylight.

entities.name: Thides I
entities.body_md: A rocky world, close to the system star, with no atmosphere. It is tidal locked, where the one side is warmed to temperatures that would boil water, while the other would freeze. The planet is made mostly of nickel and silicon with a lesser mix of other elements, primarily a source of lithium. The planet has mostly been mined out where it's gravity and structure started to break down, causing concern about the structure of the planet. Wildcatters and scavengers continue to mine out the few remaining deposits, with a hope that they don't cause the planet collapse.

entities.name: Beacon
entities.body_md: A beacon is an automated signal station placed in hyperspace to assist in navigation through hyperspace. The beacons emit a unique signal that allow ships to lock onto its location. Ships can use the signal to determine direction in hyperspace to the beacon, while using it's signal strength to determine distance. More modern ships navigation systems can use multiple signals to triangulate a rough location in physical space of the ship. Since the power and calculation requirements for gravitational navigation can be immense, many ships operate in hyperspace with beacon only navigation.

entities.name: Steiger Bulk Cargo Hauler Tug
entities.body_md: This ship designed to be a long haul tug for transporting cargo containers over long distances.

entities.name: Strider Class Transport Ship
entities.body_md:
This atmospheric enabled ship is designed for military use to land troops and supplies on planets. They are medium distance ships and are able to travel between systems and through gates, even if most are attached to larger troop carriers or supply ships. They are favored by the Federal Republican Marines due to their rugged nature and dependability.

Decommissioned Strider Class ships are desired on the resale marked due to their platform being highly customizable. You will often find these operating in inter-planetary use as well as some short to medium term hauling. Their ability to land on planets with thicker atmospheres make them particularly valuable on planets without a space elevator.

entities.name: Sentinel Class Corvette
entities.body_md: A corvette build by Steiger Aerospace, these are used by the military as scout ships, and even a few are purchased by the Marshals to help interdicting pirates. Like most Steiger builds they are rugged and highly customizable. While most of this class are space only ships, there are a view variants that are able to land on low to normal atmospheric planets.

entities.name: Diamon Mark XII Servicing Tug
entities.body_md:
A port tug used to in orbit or around stations to handle ship traffic. The tugs are built with a re-enforced forward electromagnetic hull that allows the ship to connect and push ships in port. In addition that contain a grappling arm and multiple drone docks. These ships are used to move freight and ships around the port, assist with ships navigation in port, and to conduct repairs.

Criminal elements often modify these ships for use boarding other ships.

entities.name: Horizon Aerospace ST-160
entities.body_md: An upper medium tier startship created by Horizon Aerospace. It is designed to land on planets with an atmosphere. These are used by companies that want to provide a more luxurious experience than the bear bones offered by other starships. While they are not luxury yachts, they do contain more features than some more limited designs.

entities.name: Gate
entities.body_md:
A gate, or portal, is a fixed position structure that enables ships unequipped with a vortex engine to enter into hyperspace. These gates are commonly positioned at the ends of safe hyperspace pathways to/from the system. The immense power requirements to open a portal into hyperspace limit the number of ships able to open their own access, so most ships rely on these gates to access hyperspace. Due to power requirements to open, and keep open a portal, these gates will have multiple power generation sources and storage in the immediate area.

A beacon is commonly deployed near the gate in hyperspace, to act as a lighthouse to ships.

Originally gates had to be set to open at regular intervals, due to the lack of the ability of objects in physical space being able to detect items in hyperspace. Pratrock Dynamics developed and released a new sensor array that allows this detection to occur. This advance made gate operations more efficient as they can now open on demand when ships approach.

entities.name: Vortex Engine
entities.body_md:
A vortex engine is a device used to create a portal into hyperspace. The engines are designs to generate a singularity that is able to breach physical space into hyperspace. These engines use large amounts of power, so much so that it is impractical for these engines to be deployed to most ships. Engines are more commonly found built into gates, that are able to open portals along hyperspace pathways.

The power requirements of Vortex Engines are so great, that many common power generation sources are unable to singularly create all the power at a given time. Most setups store energy in capacitors or batteries over a period of time to operate the engine. Generation level of the power source, and other power needs can effect the time needed to charge these power supplies. In addition many ships equipped with vortex engines will power down all non-essential systems when opening the portal.
